sections.heading: Basic Definitions
sections.content: Those physical quantities having both magnitude and direction and is operated by certain rules. Eg: Force, velocity, torque, displacement etc. A vector must be changed either by change of magnitude, direction or both.

definitions.Axial vectors: Those vectors related with rotation or responsible for rotation. e.g: torque, Angular momentum, angular velocity etc.
definitions.Polar vectors: Those vectors related with translation or responsible for translation. Eg. Force, displacement, linear momentum, velocity etc.
definitions.Unit vector: A vector having unit magnitude. It shows only direction. It is represented by cap over alphabet (eg. â). â = a⃗ / |a⃗| = a⃗ / a
definitions.Orthogonal unit vectors: Orthogonal means perpendicular to each other. î, ĵ, k̂ are orthogonal unit vectors. î ⇒ unit vector along X-axis, ĵ ⇒ unit vector along Y-axis, k̂ ⇒ unit vector along Z-axis

sections.heading: Component of Vector
sections.content: Resolution of vector along axes is known as component. A vector has two rectangular components in plane and has three rectangular components in space. A vector can have infinite no. of components.
formulas: If a⃗ makes an angle θ with X-axis then, its X-Component, a_x = a cos θ, Y-Component a_y = a sin θ
Vector a⃗ is written as: a⃗ = a_x î + a_y ĵ
If r⃗ = x î + y ĵ then, Magnitude of r⃗: |r⃗| = r = √(x² + y²)
Angle made by r⃗ with X-axis: θ_x = tan⁻¹(y/x)
Angle made by r⃗ with Y-axis: θ_y = tan⁻¹(x/y)
cos θ_x = x/r and cos θ_y = y/r.  cos²θ_x + cos²θ_y = 1
If r⃗ = x î + y ĵ + z k̂ then, Magnitude of r⃗: r = √(x² + y² + z²)
Angle made by r⃗ with axes: cos θ_x = x/r, cos θ_y = y/r, cos θ_z = z/r.  cos²θ_x + cos²θ_y + cos²θ_z = 1
specialCases: If a vector is equally inclined with two rectangular axes in plane then, angle made by vector with each axis is 45°.
If a vector is equally inclined with all three co-ordinate axes then angle of inclination with axis is given by θ_x = θ_y = θ_z = cos⁻¹(1/√3) = 54.74°

sections.heading: Addition of two vectors
sections.content: Resultant of two vectors lies along major diagonal of parallelogram when angle between both vector is acute (<90°). Resultant lies along minor diagonal when angle between both vector is obtuse (>90°).
formulas: Magnitude of Resultant (R) is given by: R = |R⃗| = |a⃗ + b⃗| = √(a² + b² + 2ab cos θ)
If Resultant (R) makes an angle α with a⃗, then tan α = (b sin θ) / (a + b cos θ)
If R makes an angle β with b⃗ then, tan β = (a sin θ) / (b + a cos θ)
specialCases: If a=b, R = 2a cos(θ/2) and α = β = θ/2
Condition for zero resultant from three vectors: If a⃗ + b⃗ + c⃗ = 0 then c⃗ = -(a⃗ + b⃗) ⇒ c⃗ = -R⃗ where R⃗ = a⃗ + b⃗. |c⃗| = |a⃗ + b⃗|. |a-b| ≤ c ≤ (a+b)

sections.heading: Subtraction of two vectors
sections.content: The vector which is to be subtracted is reversed.
formulas: Magnitude of (a⃗ - b⃗) is given by: |a⃗ - b⃗| = √(a² + b² - 2ab cos θ)
If a⃗ - b⃗ makes an angle α with a⃗ then: tan α = (b sin θ) / (a - b cos θ)
If a⃗ - b⃗ makes an angle β with (-b⃗) then: tan β = (a sin θ) / (b - a cos θ)
specialCases: If a=b, |a⃗ - b⃗| = 2a sin(θ/2) and α = β = 90° - θ/2
The angle between a⃗ + b⃗ and a⃗ - b⃗ is between 0° to 180°.

sections.heading: Dot product or scalar product
formulas: a⃗ . b⃗ = ab cos θ
cos θ = (a⃗ . b⃗) / (ab) = â . b̂
î.î = ĵ.ĵ = k̂.k̂ = 1
î.ĵ = ĵ.k̂ = k̂.î = 0
If a⃗ = a_x î + a_y ĵ + a_z k̂ and b⃗ = b_x î + b_y ĵ + b_z k̂ then, a⃗ . b⃗ = a_x b_x + a_y b_y + a_z b_z
Projection of b⃗ on a⃗ = (a⃗ . b⃗) / a
Projection of a⃗ on b⃗ = (a⃗ . b⃗) / b

sections.heading: Cross product or vector product
formulas: a⃗ × b⃗ = ab sin θ n̂
î × î = ĵ × ĵ = k̂ × k̂ = 0
If a⃗ = a_x î + a_y ĵ + a_z k̂ and b⃗ = b_x î + b_y ĵ + b_z k̂ then, a⃗ × b⃗ = determinant of matrix [[î, ĵ, k̂], [a_x, a_y, a_z], [b_x, b_y, b_z]]
Torque τ⃗ = r⃗ × F⃗
Angular momentum L⃗ = r⃗ × p⃗ = m(r⃗ × v⃗)
Velocity v⃗ = ω⃗ × r⃗
specialCases: The angle between a⃗ and (a⃗ × b⃗) is 90° and a⃗ . (a⃗ × b⃗) = 0
The angle between (a⃗ + b⃗) and (a⃗ × b⃗) is 90° and (a⃗ + b⃗) . (a⃗ × b⃗) = 0
The angle between (a⃗ - b⃗) and (a⃗ × b⃗) is 90° and (a⃗ - b⃗) . (a⃗ × b⃗) = 0
If d₁ and d₂ are two diagonals of parallelogram then, Area of parallelogram = 1/2 |d₁ × d₂|
